content.text: The Polyglot Atlas v2 separates structure from aesthetics. You can control the 'feel' of the interface through three different layers of customization.
The Style Picker in the navbar controls the physical properties of all components globally. These are driven by CSS variables like --radius and --pad.

items: ⬚ Sharp: Zero border-radius, thin borders, compact padding. Best for a terminal/IDE look.
◯ Round: Standard 12px radius, soft shadows. The default modern experience.
☁ Airy: 24px radius, double padding, and heavy shadows. Designed for high-readability focus.

content.text: As seen in this guide, setting a preset at the top of the JSON file changes the default appearance of all nested content.
Setting 'style': 'text_style' in the root JSON object makes all blocks render without borders, resembling a documentation article.
Individual blocks can break away from the page preset by defining their own style property.
Even though this page uses text_style, this specific block has its style set to empty (default), so it appears as a standard bordered card.
Independent of the visual style, the Detailed / Compact toggle in the right sidebar controls how many levels of the Table of Contents are visible.

items: Detailed: Shows every nested sub-topic in the sidebar.
Compact: Hides everything below Level 1 for a cleaner navigation experience.
